prerequisites: A Freshservice account with access to your support portal.
Your Freshservice domain name (for example acmeinc.freshservice.com), used as the base for all API calls (https://your_domain/api/v2/...).
Your personal Freshservice API key, found in Profile settings (top-right profile picture > Profile settings), shown to the right of the profile details.
A start date marking the earliest point from which to sync records.

authMethods.name: API key (HTTP Basic auth)
authMethods.summary: Freshservice authenticates with your personal API key over HTTP Basic authentication. The key is sent as the username and any dummy value (for example X) is used as the password, so no real password is required. Provide this value in the connector's api_key field; the domain_name field determines the request base URL (https://your_domain/api/v2/...).

setupSteps.title: Find your API key
setupSteps.body: Log into your Freshservice support portal, click your profile picture in the top-right corner, and open Profile settings. Your API key is displayed to the right of your profile details. Copy it for use as the connector's api_key.

setupSteps.title: Identify your domain name
setupSteps.body: Note your Freshservice domain (for example acmeinc.freshservice.com). Freshservice API requests are made against https://your_domain/api/v2/, so this value is required to route requests to your account.

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector
setupSteps.body: Enter the api_key you copied, set domain_name to your Freshservice domain, and set start_date to the earliest date from which records should be synced. The connector authenticates by passing the API key as the Basic-auth username with a dummy password.

sources.title: Where do I find my API key? - Freshservice Support
